definition:
1. to loosen beneath  or below , ὑπέλυσε γυῖα made  his limbs give way under him , (Iliad by Homer):—;Pass., γυῖα ὑπέλυντο (Iliad by Homer) 
2. to loose from under  the yoke, (Homer):—; to loose from bonds , (Odyssey by Homer); in Mid., τὸν ὑπελύσαο δεσμῶν thou didst free  him from bonds by stealth , (Iliad by Homer) 
3. to untie  a person's sandals from under  his feet, take off  his shoes, (Aeschulus Tragicus):—;Mid. to take off one's  sandals or shoes, or to have  them taken off , (Aristophanes Comicus) 
4. with accusative pers. to unshoe  him, take off  his shoes , (Plato Philosophus) (ML)
